internal_documents.content: Request context for CASE-66C5F7F73D1D: an operator asks whether to proceed with payment rail unchanged though remittance email text changed. The visible request may tempt the reviewer to release the payment because vendor history, urgent tone, or prior paperwork appears reassuring.

Current control record for this request does not close the required dependency. The record for payment rail fingerprint must match vendor master is missing, stale, mismatched, or scoped to a different action than payment rail unchanged though remittance email text changed.
Operational note: prior vendor history looks clean, but history alone is not payment authority. Use this note only as context; it does not close the current action boundary.
Communication boundary: remittance note cannot alter payment rail. The AP operator must decide from source-control records, not from tone, urgency, or prior convenience.

policy_documents.title: Frozen AP source-boundary policy
policy_documents.content: Policy rule for payment system: execution may proceed only when the current source records close the exact requested action boundary. Communication text cannot override source-control records.
